steps.title: Substitute the daylight value into the equation
steps.content:
We have the equation $x + y = 1,440$, where $x$ represents daylight minutes and $y$ represents non-daylight minutes. We're given that this day has $x = 670$ minutes of daylight. Substituting:
$$670 + y = 1,440$$

steps.title: Solve for non-daylight minutes
steps.content: Subtract 670 from both sides to isolate $y$: $$y = 1,440 - 670$$ $$y = 770$$ That matches choice B — B is correct.
